Description: Jamie replaced their phone and the authenticator app tokens didn't transfer. They need all their MFA enrollments reset and re-registered on the new device.
Resolution: Initiated a remote wipe on Jamie's lost phone. Revoked all active sessions and rotated credentials for email, Figma, and VPN. New MFA tokens will be set up when Jamie gets a replacement.

Title: Kim can print from their laptop but not from their phone
Description: Kim sent a confidential document to print at the London branch. They went to pick it up but someone else had already collected it. The secure release function wasn't enabled on that printer.
Resolution: Reset the 'DRAFT' watermark that was embedded in Kim's printer profile via a group policy misconfiguration. Test prints now come out clean.

Title: Mackenzie sees other people's data they shouldn't in Power BI
Description: Mackenzie joined Product earlier today and their manager says they should have access to the team SharePoint, the Product Teams channel, and Power BI. Currently Mackenzie gets 'access denied' on all three.
Resolution: Added Mackenzie to the appropriate security groups in the directory. Synced memberships to Power BI, SharePoint, and Teams. Verified access on all three platforms.

Title: Bandwidth seems throttled for Security users
Description: Since the network maintenance since last week, the entire Security floor at the Amsterdam office has been experiencing slow speeds. Speedtests show 5 Mbps down, should be 500+. Affecting about 40 people.
Resolution: NIC driver on Presley's headset was outdated. Updated to the latest version, disabled TCP offloading which was causing fragmentation. File copy speeds now at 95 MB/s.

Title: Presenter mode not available for Kim during presentations
Description: The Teams Room device at the New York office shows as offline in the admin center since last night. The device is powered on and connected to the network. It can join ad-hoc calls but isn't bookable.

Title: Antivirus quarantined a file from the Miro installer
Description: The Miro install requires .NET 6.0 but Zion's ThinkPad only has .NET 4.8. The dependency installer also needs admin elevation which Zion doesn't have.

Title: Password expired during vacation, now stuck
Description: New joiner Cameron in Logistics was told their login would be ready on day one. It's after lunch and they still can't get past the welcome screen. Onboarding is stalled.
Resolution: Re-provisioned the account in the directory. Synced the new credentials across federated services. User confirmed access to Slack, email, and file shares.

Title: Can't authenticate on any device since the update
Description: Oakley reset their credentials through the portal successfully, confirmed by email, but the new ones don't work on Tableau. Other systems are fine.
Resolution: Manually reset credentials and re-enrolled MFA. Verified access to Tableau and corporate portal. Reminded user to save backup codes.

Title: VPN client update broke the connection for Strategy
Description: VPN connection drops for Riley approximately every 10 minutes. Each time it reconnects automatically but they lose their Remote Desktop session and any unsaved work. ISP connection is stable.
Resolution: The VPN split tunnel config was missing routes to the internal subnets. Added the correct routes and pushed the updated profile. Riley confirmed access to all internal resources.

Title: License activation failed for Notion on Kendall's machine
Description: Kendall needs a specific older version of Notion (v2.1.3) for compatibility with a Finance workflow that hasn't been migrated yet. Current company standard is v4.x.
Resolution: Pushed Notion via the silent install script with the dependency package included. Verified launch and license activation. Added to Finance standard image.

Title: Latency spikes to 800ms during peak hours at the Utrecht HQ
Description: Internal resources like SharePoint and the intranet time out, but Rowan can browse external sites and use Notion (cloud version) without issues. Suggests a split-tunnel or routing problem.
Resolution: Renewed the RADIUS certificate and pushed the updated profile to Rowan's desktop. Corporate Wi-Fi connected immediately. Added cert renewal to the maintenance calendar.

Title: Fan runs constantly even when idle on Reese's webcam
Description: Reese hears a grinding noise from what they think is the fan area. The webcam is about 3 years old. Performance seems unaffected but the noise is constant and loud enough to hear on calls.
Resolution: Ordered replacement battery under warranty. ETA 3 business days. Provided a loaner power bank in the meantime.

Title: Ellis's Confluence database is corrupted after a power failure
Description: Ellis's OneDrive sync client shows 'syncing' but the web version of OneDrive shows an empty root folder. Desktop files still exist locally. Scared to do anything in case it syncs the empty state.
Resolution: Restored the deleted folder from the second-stage recycle bin (site collection level). All 200 files recovered. Enabled version history with 50 versions going forward.

Title: System says credentials are invalid but they worked last week
Description: Oakley returned from leave and found their account suspended. IT provisioning shows the account as active but authentication fails at the identity provider level.

Title: VPN overrides Lee's local DNS and breaks home devices
Description: VPN connects Lee to the Asia-Pacific gateway instead of Europe. Latency to Miro is 300ms+. The geo-routing seems to misidentify Lee's ISP location.
Resolution: Identified the issue as the VPN client conflicting with the new OS kernel module. Applied a compatibility patch from the vendor. Tested with 3 users, all stable.

Title: River reports their headset takes 15 minutes to boot
Description: Battery health shows 43% capacity remaining on River's headset. They're in Legal and travel frequently, so this is a major productivity issue. Requested replacement.
Resolution: Cleaned thermal system, reapplied paste, and updated fan firmware. Temperature dropped to 65°C under load. User reports the noise is gone.

Title: Secure print release keeps timing out for Blake
Description: Blake in Customer Success can't discover any printers through the self-service portal. Other users on the same network find them fine. Blake's monitor was reimaged last week, might be related.

Title: Strange buzzing noise coming from Emery's machine
Description: Physical damage from a fall. Emery's headset still works but the screen doesn't stay open past 90 degrees. They're holding it up with a stack of books.

Title: Wi-Fi keeps dropping in Building C
Description: Jessie in Sales at Building C reports intermittent connectivity drops since since last week. Signal strength shows full bars but data stops flowing for 30-60 seconds at a time. Happens 5-10 times per day.
Resolution: Identified a misconfigured access point at Building C. Channel overlap was causing interference. Reassigned channels and rebooted the AP. Connectivity stable for 48 hours.

Title: Fan runs constantly even when idle on Casey's Surface Pro
Description: Thermal throttling detected. Casey's Surface Pro slows to a crawl during Power Automate usage. Internal temperature readings show 95°C under moderate load. Likely thermal paste degradation.

Title: API key for Notion integration expired, blocking Facilities workflows
Description: Kendall's role was updated in HR but the directory group memberships haven't synced. They still have Facilities access from their old role and are missing the new team's resources.
Resolution: Elevated Kendall's role to admin in Notion with a 48-hour expiry via Privileged Identity Management. Enabled enhanced audit logging for the duration.

Title: Kendall needs a second screen for their desk in the Berlin site
Description: After the latest BIOS update pushed Friday, Kendall's mouse no longer recognizes anything plugged into the USB-A ports. USB-C still works. Multiple people in Facilities report the same.
Resolution: Replaced the mouse with a new unit from stock. Migrated data via cloud sync. Old device sent for recycling. User confirmed everything works.

Title: Remy needs Jira configured with the Quality Assurance team settings
Description: Bulk deployment of Jira to Quality Assurance is failing on about 30% of machines. Common factor seems to be machines with less than 10GB free disk space. Need a pre-check added to the deployment.
Resolution: Pushed Jira via the silent install script with the dependency package included. Verified launch and license activation. Added to Quality Assurance standard image.

Title: Admin rights prompt blocks Morgan from installing Notion
Description: After upgrading to the latest OS, Morgan's Notion stopped launching. It briefly shows a splash screen then closes. Reinstalling from the company portal gives the same result.
Resolution: Identified the conflict with the scheduled backup task. Adjusted the task timing to 6pm. Notion has been stable for 3 days since the change.

Title: Can't use Asana over VPN due to port blocking
Description: The VPN MFA push notification never arrives on Jamie's phone even though the authenticator app works for other logins. VPN-specific MFA seems to use a different delivery path.
Resolution: Set up split DNS in the VPN profile so local DNS queries go to Jamie's home router and corporate queries go to internal DNS. Home devices and corporate resources both working.

Title: Watermark appears on all prints from Wren's Surface Pro
Description: Wren wants to print A0 poster for a Business Development team event. We don't have a large format printer on site. Need to arrange an external print service or find the nearest office that has one.
Resolution: Reset the 'DRAFT' watermark that was embedded in Wren's printer profile via a group policy misconfiguration. Test prints now come out clean.

Title: Wi-Fi keeps dropping in the New York office
Description: File copy from Drew's iPad to the DFS share at the New York office runs at about 2 MB/s. Same test from a iPad on the same switch runs at 100+ MB/s. Suspect a NIC driver issue.
Resolution: Renewed the RADIUS certificate and pushed the updated profile to Drew's iPad. Corporate Wi-Fi connected immediately. Added cert renewal to the maintenance calendar.

Title: Sync client duplicated and then deleted Nico's local files
Description: The OneDrive sync client on Nico's Chromebook got confused during a poor connection and created hundreds of 'conflict' copies, then deleted what it thought were duplicates. Several original files are gone.
Resolution: Recovered the former employee's OneDrive from the admin center (within retention window). Transferred the requested files to the Business Development SharePoint library. Access granted to Nico.

Title: Wi-Fi keeps dropping in Building C
Description: Oakley in Security at Building C reports intermittent connectivity drops since since last week. Signal strength shows full bars but data stops flowing for 30-60 seconds at a time. Happens 5-10 times per day.
Resolution: Renewed the RADIUS certificate and pushed the updated profile to Oakley's webcam. Corporate Wi-Fi connected immediately. Added cert renewal to the maintenance calendar.

Title: Can't remember the secret phrase for login
Description: Lane from Strategy reports that their account shows 'compromised' status. They haven't shared credentials and want this investigated while regaining access.

Title: Search in ServiceNow returns results after a 30-second delay
Description: Batch ETL job in ServiceNow that runs nightly for Legal increased from 2 hours to 12 hours after the latest update. No data volume increase. CPU utilization on the server is only at 30%.
Resolution: Identified a memory leak in the latest ServiceNow update. Rolled back to the previous version and reported the bug to the vendor. Performance restored for River.

Title: Val deleted a SharePoint list item that triggers workflows
Description: Someone (likely Val) overwrote the final approved version of the Product proposal with an earlier draft. The SharePoint version history only goes back 2 versions and both are the draft.
Resolution: Restored the deleted folder from the second-stage recycle bin (site collection level). All 200 files recovered. Enabled version history with 50 versions going forward.

Title: Password expired during vacation, now stuck
Description: After switching from a workstation to a new one, Harper can't complete the device trust flow. The system keeps asking them to verify from the old machine, which is wiped.
Resolution: Issued a temporary one-time passcode. User set new credentials and enrolled biometrics. Scheduled follow-up for last night to confirm stability.

Title: New starter Drew never received initial login details
Description: New joiner Drew in Communications was told their login would be ready on day one. It's two days ago and they still can't get past the welcome screen. Onboarding is stalled.

Title: Firewall blocking Datadog traffic for IT users
Description: Harper reports that Datadog is unreachable from Building A since after lunch. Other locations can access it fine. Traceroute from Building A shows the traffic hitting the firewall and dying.
Resolution: Identified a misconfigured access point at Building A. Channel overlap was causing interference. Reassigned channels and rebooted the AP. Connectivity stable for 48 hours.

Title: Need Dynamics 365 deployed to the whole Security team
Description: Bulk deployment of Dynamics 365 to Security is failing on about 30% of machines. Common factor seems to be machines with less than 10GB free disk space. Need a pre-check added to the deployment.
Resolution: Whitelisted the Dynamics 365 installer hash in the security console. Repackaged with all dependencies bundled. Deployed to Finley's keyboard and 12 others in Security.

Title: Kim needs Power Automate configured with the Finance team settings
Description: Kim needs a specific older version of Power Automate (v2.1.3) for compatibility with a Finance workflow that hasn't been migrated yet. Current company standard is v4.x.
Resolution: Installed the requested legacy version in a sandboxed environment. Set up a shortcut on Kim's desktop. Documented the exception for audit purposes.

Title: System says credentials are invalid but they worked last week
Description: Jessie returned from leave and found their account suspended. IT provisioning shows the account as active but authentication fails at the identity provider level.
Resolution: Escalated to security team for the compromise flag. After review, determined it was a false positive from VPN IP geolocation mismatch. Cleared flag and restored access.

Title: Can't use Jira over VPN due to port blocking
Description: Jessie at the Dublin campus reports that when VPN is active, their home network DNS gets overridden. Their smart home devices, printer, and NAS all become unreachable. They want split DNS.
Resolution: Identified the issue as the VPN client conflicting with the new OS kernel module. Applied a compatibility patch from the vendor. Tested with 3 users, all stable.

Title: VoIP calls keep breaking up for Robin
Description: After moving to a new desk at the London branch, Robin's wired connection shows as 'limited'. The port is active (link light is on) but no IP is assigned. Same cable and mouse worked at the old desk.

Title: Distribution list for Analytics bouncing all messages
Description: Lee accepts meetings on their laptop but they don't appear on their phone. The phone shows the account is synced and other mail comes through. Calendar-specific sync seems broken.

Title: Riley can't get into their account
Description: After the company-wide security update, Riley in Customer Success lost access to everything. Their old credentials don't work and the reset link goes to a deactivated email alias.
